headline: First Atmosphere Found on Rocky Planet in Habitable Zone
meta_description: Astronomers detect the first confirmed atmosphere around a rocky exoplanet in another star's habitable zone, marking a major breakthrough in the search for lif…
summary_short: For the first time, astronomers have confirmed an atmosphere around a rocky planet orbiting in another star's habitable zone—a milestone in humanity's search for potenti…
body:
For the first time, astronomers have confirmed the existence of an atmosphere around a rocky planet located in another star's habitable zone. The planet, LHS 1140 b, sits approximately 48 light-years from Earth and orbits a red dwarf star in the region where liquid water could potentially exist on a planetary surface. The discovery, published in *Science* in July 2026, reveals that at least one rocky, Earth-like world has managed to preserve its atmosphere for more than three billion years—offering the strongest evidence yet that planets with conditions suitable for life may be common beyond our solar system.

The breakthrough came through an unexpected method. Harvard researcher Collin Cherubim and his team detected helium slowly leaking from LHS 1140 b's upper atmosphere into space. Using the Warm Infrared Echelle Spectrograph at the Magellan Observatory in Chile, the astronomers observed the planet during a rare transit when it and another planet crossed in front of their star on the same night. While that companion planet showed no atmospheric signature, LHS 1140 b produced a clear, statistically solid detection of escaping helium—confirming theoretical predictions that had never before been validated from a rocky world.

The finding opens an unexpected path forward. Rather than relying solely on space telescopes, astronomers may now search for rocky exoplanet atmospheres from the ground by looking for gases escaping into space. Cherubim plans to determine the complete chemical composition of LHS 1140 b's atmosphere and investigate whether surface oceans or other features linked to habitability exist there. "This has been a model validation, and hopefully it's just the first of many more observations to come," he said.
quick_facts: First confirmed atmosphere on a rocky planet in a habitable zone
Planet LHS 1140 b located 48 light-years from Earth
Atmosphere may have survived for over 3 billion years
Discovery made using helium detection from Chile observatory
